name: Strawberry-Banana Tarts
recipeInstructions:
1. Grease six 6 oz. custard cups; set aside. Unfold phyllo dough. Remove one sheet, keeping the rest covered with plastic wrap and a damp towel. Place the first sheet of phyllo on a waxed paper-lined cutting board. Brush with some of the melted butter. Top with a second sheet of phyllo, then brush with more butter. Repeat layering with the remaining phyllo and butter.
2. Cut the stack crosswise into six 3-inch wide strips. Cut lengthwise into thirds to form 18 4x3 inch rectangles. Press three rectangles into each custard cup with shorter edges overlapping in the bottoms of cups. Place the cups in a 15x10x1 inch baking pan. Sprinkle sugar and cinnamon over phyllo.
3. Bake in a 350°F oven for 15 to 18 minutes, or until golden. Cool in the cups.
4. For filling, in a medium mixing bowl combine whipping cream, powdered sugar, orange peel, and vanilla. Beat with an electric mixer on low speed just until stiff peaks form. Fold in sour cream. Set aside 3/4 cup of mixture.
5. To assemble, carefully lift baked phyllo shells from the custard cups and place them on a serving plate. Spoon about 1/4 cup of the filling into each shell. Top with strawberry and banana slices. Spoon or pipe a rounded tablespoon of the remaining filling on top of each tart. Makes 6.
